title: Snake & Ladder Upgrades + Realistic Player Car & Snake Visuals

requirements.text: Add a spinning/tumbling dice roll animation to the Snake and Ladder game at /snake-ladder. When the Roll Dice button is pressed, the dice element must visually spin (CSS keyframe rotation or 3D tumble) for approximately 0.8–1 second before settling on the rolled value.
acceptanceCriteria: Pressing Roll Dice triggers a visible spinning animation on the dice element.
The animation lasts approximately 0.8–1 second before the final value is shown.
The board and turn logic do not advance until the animation completes.
The animation uses CSS keyframes or JS-driven transforms with no external assets.

requirements.text: Animate player token movement square by square in the Snake and Ladder game at /snake-ladder. When a player rolls the dice, their token must visually move one cell at a time along the board path with smooth easing between each step.

quotes: Animate player token movement square by square.

acceptanceCriteria: After a dice roll, the token moves one square at a time with a visible transition between cells.
The movement easing is smooth (e.g., ease-in-out) and not instantaneous.
The token reaches the correct destination cell after completing all steps.
Snake/ladder logic fires only after the token finishes its square-by-square movement.

requirements.text: Add a sliding down animation to the Snake and Ladder game at /snake-ladder when a player lands on a snake head. The token must visually animate downward from the snake's head position to the snake's tail position before the turn ends.

quotes: Add sliding animation when hitting snake.

acceptanceCriteria: When a token lands on a snake head cell, a downward sliding animation plays.
The token transitions visually from the head cell to the tail cell with smooth motion.
The animation is distinct from regular movement (e.g., curved path or CSS translate).
Turn does not end until the snake slide animation completes.

requirements.text: Add a climbing up animation to the Snake and Ladder game at /snake-ladder when a player lands on a ladder bottom. The token must visually animate upward from the ladder's base cell to the ladder's top cell before the turn ends.

quotes: Add climbing animation when using ladder.

acceptanceCriteria: When a token lands on a ladder base cell, an upward climbing animation plays.
The token transitions visually from the base cell to the top cell with smooth motion.
The animation is visually distinct from regular movement.
Turn does not end until the ladder climbing animation completes.

requirements.text: Add a confetti burst effect to the Snake and Ladder game at /snake-ladder when a player wins. When a player's token reaches square 100, a full-screen confetti animation must play over the winner announcement overlay.

quotes: Add confetti effect when someone wins.

acceptanceCriteria: Winning triggers a confetti particle burst that covers the game area or full screen.
Confetti particles use multiple neon colors consistent with the dark neon theme.
The confetti animation plays simultaneously with or just after the winner announcement.
Confetti is implemented with CSS/JS — no external libraries or assets.

requirements.text: Add a glowing turn indicator to the Snake and Ladder game at /snake-ladder. The current player's name or token label must be highlighted with a neon pulsing glow effect to clearly show whose turn it is. The glow must switch to the other player immediately after a turn ends.
acceptanceCriteria: The active player's turn indicator displays a neon pulsing glow animation.
The glow switches to the other player's indicator after each turn completes.
The inactive player's indicator has no glow or a dim state.
The indicator is visually prominent and readable on the dark neon theme.

requirements.text: Add Web Audio API sound effects (programmatically generated, no external audio files) to the Snake and Ladder game at /snake-ladder. Play distinct sounds for: dice roll (short percussive rattle), snake slide (descending pitch tone), ladder climb (ascending pitch tone), and winner fanfare (triumphant ascending chord sequence).

quotes: Add sound effects for dice, snake, ladder, and win.

acceptanceCriteria: A short percussive rattle sound plays when the dice roll animation begins.
A descending tone plays when a player slides down a snake.
An ascending tone plays when a player climbs a ladder.
A triumphant fanfare sound plays when a player wins.
All sounds are generated via Web Audio API with no external audio files.
Sounds play in sync with the corresponding animations.

requirements.text: Redesign the player car in the Traffic Car Game at /traffic to look more realistic and detailed. Replace the current basic shape with a multi-layered CSS/canvas car illustration that includes: a car body with visible roof and hood sections, side windows, front windshield, headlights with a glow effect, visible wheels on both sides, and a distinct color scheme that stands out on the road.

quotes: traffic car game player car make more real

acceptanceCriteria: The player car displays a recognizable car silhouette with roof, hood, and body sections.
Front headlights are rendered with a neon or bright glow effect.
Side windows and a windshield are visually distinguishable on the car body.
Wheels are visible on both the left and right sides of the car.
The car uses a distinct color scheme that contrasts with the road background.
The car retains its existing hitbox dimensions so collision detection is unaffected.
All rendering is done via canvas draw calls or layered CSS — no external image assets.

requirements.text: Redesign the snake in the Snake Game at /snake to look more realistic and detailed. Replace the current basic rectangle segments with a segmented snake that includes: a distinct head shape with two visible eyes and a tongue, gradient-colored body segments that taper slightly toward the tail, a curved/rounded segment style to give a lifelike serpentine appearance, and a pointed or tapered tail segment.

quotes: snake game player snake make real

acceptanceCriteria: The snake head is visually distinct from body segments, with two small eyes rendered.
A small tongue or forked tip is drawn on the head pointing in the movement direction.
Body segments use a gradient fill (e.g., green shades) that creates a scale-like or rounded appearance.
Segments near the tail are slightly smaller or tapered to suggest a tail end.
The final tail segment is pointed or visually tapered.
The snake head rotates or is oriented to face the current movement direction.
All rendering is done via canvas draw calls — no external image assets.
Existing game logic, speed increases, collision detection, and scoring are unaffected.

constraints: All animations and sound effects must use CSS keyframes, JS/canvas, or Web Audio API only — no external audio files, image assets, or third-party animation libraries.
Existing game logic, collision detection, scoring, localStorage persistence, and Back to Home buttons must remain intact in both TrafficCarGame and SnakeGame.
Snake and Ladder existing board layout, snakes/ladders positions, restart button, and Back to Home button must be preserved.
Player car hitbox dimensions in TrafficCarGame must not change so existing collision detection continues to work correctly.
Snake segment hitbox/grid logic in SnakeGame must not change so existing collision detection and food spawning continue to work correctly.
nonGoals: Adding new game modes or levels to any game.
Changing the board layout or snake/ladder positions in Snake and Ladder.
Changing any route paths or navigation structure.
Redesigning traffic car sprites (non-player cars) or Snake game food items.
Adding new sound effects to Traffic Car Game or Snake Game beyond what already exists.
